channel.title: Posts on The Cogitating Ceviche

item.title: The Intern Now Has API Keys
item.description:
A small crowd of permissions has gathered inside your CRM, and nobody remembers hiring it.Shadow IT used to be legible. A person in marketing signed up for Dropbox because the approved file-sharing solution had all the speed and charm of a county…

item.title: The Mercy of Ordinary Rules
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowMost people can recognize kindness when it arrives as warmth, welcome, patience, or relief. A meal brought to the sick is easy to call merciful. So is a debt forgiven, a frightened child held, a lonely person…

item.title: The Compliance Watermark
item.description:
The old internet asked whether something was true. That was not always a sophisticated question, but at least it had a certain peasant honesty. Did the thing happen? Did the person say it? Is the photograph real? Is the document authentic? Is the…

item.title: Emily Dickinson: A Voice Folded Inward
item.description:
The Poems Went Without HerIn February 1866, readers of the Springfield Daily Republican found a poem titled “The Snake.” Its author was not named. She had not sent it, had not titled it, and did not want it there. The poem was Emily Dickinson’s “A…

item.title: Tokenized Everything and the Bureaucratization of Magic Beans
item.description:

item.description:

item.title: Nikola Tesla: When Vision Could Not Become Institution
item.description:

item.title: The Heat Must Go Somewhere
item.description:

item.title: Algorithmic Feudalism

item.title: The Cloud Has Plumbing
item.description:
